feed.title: Scholar Alerts — Clean Feed
feed.subtitle: Google Scholar alerts reformatted as individual article entries

entry.title: Quantum‐Dot‐Mediated Light Field Imaging by Photocurrent Scanning Force Microscopy
entry.content:
Authors: F Küstner, A Hohenau, H Ditlbacher, JR Krenn - Nanophotonics, 2026

Abstract: Light field distributions on sample surfaces are mapped by detecting the photocurrents locally induced in a monolayer of colloidal perovskite‐capped PbS quantum dots with a conductive scanning force microsope. The signal levels depend ...

Alert: Joachim R Krenn (author alert)

entry.title: Magnetic flux systems involved in the May 2024 solar energetic events from AR 13664 inferred through coronal dimmings
entry.content:
Authors: A Razquin, K Dissauer, AM Veronig, G Barnes - arXiv preprint arXiv:2603.23623, 2026

Abstract: Coronal dimmings are transient depletions of coronal plasma observed in extreme ultraviolet and soft X-rays, interpreted as low-corona signatures of coronal mass ejections (CMEs). Their evolution is closely linked to CME dynamics, flare ...

Alert: Astrid Veronig (author alert)

entry.title: Dogs were widely distributed across western Eurasia during the Palaeolithic
entry.content:
Authors: WA Marsh, L Scarsbrook, E Yüncü, L Hodgson, AT Lin... - Nature, 2026

Abstract: Archaeological evidence suggests that dogs diverged from wolves during the Palaeolithic, more than 15,000 years ago,,,,,–. The earliest unequivocal genetic evidence, however, is associated with dog remains from Mesolithic archaeological ...

Alert: Tom Higham (author alert)

entry.title: Cooperation by non-kin during birth underpins sperm whale social complexity
entry.content:
Authors: A Maalouf, J DelPreto, M Lucas, S Poetto, J Andreas... - Science, 2026

Abstract: We quantitatively document a sperm whale birth event, revealing collective support behaviors across kinship lines. Using high-resolution drone footage, computer vision, and multiscale network analysis, we studied the interactions within a ...

Alert: Giovanni Petri (author alert)

entry.title: Description of a collaborative sperm whale birth and shifts in coda vocal styles during key events
entry.content:
Authors: Y Aluma, Z Baron, R Barrett, C Baumgartner, G Beguš... - Scientific Reports, 2026

Abstract: Wild cetacean birth observations are extremely rare, with observations having been recorded in less than 10% of cetacean species. Here, we describe a detailed accounting of a sperm whale (Physeter macrocephalus) birth off the coast of ...

Alert: Giovanni Petri (author alert)
